message: test(fuzz): add configured resp sequence harness (br-frankenredis-hx4n)

files.patch:
@@ -345,7 +345,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenredis-hs04.3","title":"Benchmark + conformance gate for HashMap migration","description":"# Benchmark + Conformance Gate for HashMap Migration

## What

After frankenredis-hs04.2 (HashMap implementation) is complete, run the full
quality gate and produce a before/after delta artifact.

## Steps

1. Build release binary with HashMap changes
2. Run fr-bench for all workloads: set, get, incr, mixed (p1 and p16)
3. Capture perf flamegraph to confirm BTreeMap::insert and memcmp overhead
   is reduced
4. Run cargo test --workspace (all unit tests)
5. Run cargo test -p fr-conformance (all 43 conformance tests)
6. Verify SCAN works correctly with a multi-cursor full-scan test
7. Compare throughput numbers to the April 9 baseline
8. Write isomorphism proof artifact at
   artifacts/optimization/hashmap-migration/ISOMORPHISM_PROOF.md

## Expected Outcome

BTreeMap::insert (2.93%) and __memcmp_avx2_movbe (12.73%) should both drop
significantly. Combined ~15% CPU savings → estimated 10-15% throughput
improvement. SET p1 should move from 75K to ~85K+ ops/sec.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 43 conformance tests pass
2. All workspace tests pass
3. Benchmark shows measurable improvement (>5% on SET)
4. Flamegraph confirms BTreeMap is no longer in top 10 CPU consumers
5. Isomorphism proof artifact is written and checked in
6. README updated with new numbers if improvement is significant","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:11.895427599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:20:34.479134166Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:20:34.478867962Z","close_reason":"Benchmark verified: SET p1=66K (70% Redis), SET p16=257K (30% Redis), all 43 conformance tests pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","perf","store"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.3","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hs04","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:11.895427599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-hs04.3","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-hs04.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:27.573416302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-htt7","title":"Expand live Redis parity coverage for stable CONFIG admin surface","description":"Add a broader stable core_config live differential subset for deterministic CONFIG GET/pattern/wrong-arity/case-insensitive/hz-admin behavior against vendored Redis without overlapping FUNCTION or ACL follow-up work.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T21:08:19.223225232Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T21:12:45.307012473Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T21:12:45.305182069Z","close_reason":"Expanded stable live Redis CONFIG admin-surface coverage in standalone mode","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-hvfa","title":"Track real aof_enabled INFO persistence state","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T15:51:12.331027440Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T15:53:43.214620867Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T15:53:43.214342210Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankenredis-hx4n","title":"Add structured fr-protocol fuzz target for parser limits and RESP frame sequences","description":"Evidence: fuzz_resp_parser.rs currently throws raw bytes at parse_frame/parse_frame_with_config, and fuzz_resp_roundtrip.rs only checks encode/decode identity. That leaves a gap around structure-aware parser-limit boundaries and multi-frame consumed-length invariants in crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs:128 (parse_frame), 132 (parse_frame_with_config), 140 (parse_frame_internal), 180 (parse_bulk), and 211 (parse_array).\n\nNeed: add a structure-aware libFuzzer target that generates valid and near-valid RESP scalars/arrays plus trailing frame sequences, varies ParserConfig limits, and asserts invariants: consumed bytes stop exactly at the first frame, accepted frames stay accepted under looser limits, rejected frames at tight limits never panic, and back-to-back encoded frames can be iteratively parsed without drift.\n\nReal impl path: fuzz/Cargo.toml plus new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_resp_configured_sequences.rs hitting fr-protocol directly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:17:19.110653598Z","created_by":"cod-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:17:19.110653598Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fr-protocol","fuzz","testing"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-hx4n","title":"Add structured fr-protocol fuzz target for parser limits and RESP frame sequences","description":"Evidence: fuzz_resp_parser.rs currently throws raw bytes at parse_frame/parse_frame_with_config, and fuzz_resp_roundtrip.rs only checks encode/decode identity. That leaves a gap around structure-aware parser-limit boundaries and multi-frame consumed-length invariants in crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs:128 (parse_frame), 132 (parse_frame_with_config), 140 (parse_frame_internal), 180 (parse_bulk), and 211 (parse_array).\n\nNeed: add a structure-aware libFuzzer target that generates valid and near-valid RESP scalars/arrays plus trailing frame sequences, varies ParserConfig limits, and asserts invariants: consumed bytes stop exactly at the first frame, accepted frames stay accepted under looser limits, rejected frames at tight limits never panic, and back-to-back encoded frames can be iteratively parsed without drift.\n\nReal impl path: fuzz/Cargo.toml plus new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_resp_configured_sequences.rs hitting fr-protocol directly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:17:19.110653598Z","created_by":"cod-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:34:26Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:34:26Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fr-protocol","fuzz","testing"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-hzb","title":"Wire live replica PSYNC socket ingest into runtime apply path","description":"Runtime now has apply_replication_sync_payload() and PSYNC reply parsing, but there is still no outbound replica connection / handshake / payload reader that talks to a primary over TCP and feeds FULLRESYNC/CONTINUE payloads into the runtime. Implement the live replica sync loop and state transitions using the existing fr-repl/runtime seams.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T16:09:11.095071728Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:16:24.420133527Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T16:16:24.419741274Z","close_reason":"Implemented live one-shot replica PSYNC handshake and FULLRESYNC socket ingest into runtime apply path","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
